description:
You are assisting a senior technical-writer who is reorganising several tab-separated reference tables.  
Three existing TSV files already sit in the writer’s workspace:

1. /home/user/documents/drafts/chapter_data.tsv  
2. /home/user/documents/drafts/author_data.tsv  
3. /home/user/documents/drafts/release_data.tsv  

Every file has exactly four lines: a header row plus three data rows.  
The files use the Unix LF line ending and a single horizontal TAB (0x09) as the field-separator.

Exact current contents:

• /home/user/documents/drafts/chapter_data.tsv  
  (columns = ChapterID, Seq, Title)  
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
  ChapterID<TAB>Seq<TAB>Title  
  CH01<TAB>1<TAB>Introduction  
  CH02<TAB>2<TAB>Installation  
  CH03<TAB>3<TAB>Configuration  

• /home/user/documents/drafts/author_data.tsv  
  (columns = ID, AuthorName, Email)  
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
  ID<TAB>AuthorName<TAB>Email  
  11<TAB>Alice Johnson<TAB>alice@example.com  
  12<TAB>Bob Smith<TAB>bob@example.com  
  13<TAB>Carol Chen<TAB>carol@example.com  

• /home/user/documents/drafts/release_data.tsv  
  (columns = Ref, Stage, TargetDate)  
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
  Ref<TAB>Stage<TAB>TargetDate  
  a<TAB>draft<TAB>2023-10-01  
  b<TAB>review<TAB>2023-10-15  
  c<TAB>final<TAB>2023-11-01  

Task you must complete:

A. Build a new consolidated file called /home/user/documents/final/release_master.tsv that satisfies ALL of the following rules:

   1. The directory /home/user/documents/final/ must be created if it does not already exist.  
   2. The first line of release_master.tsv is the header EXACTLY as shown below (with TABs):  
      ChapterID<TAB>Title<TAB>AuthorName<TAB>TargetDate  
   3. Rows 2-4 of release_master.tsv are formed by cutting and pasting columns from the three originals **in parallel, line-for-line** (ignore each file’s header line when merging):  
      • From chapter_data.tsv take column 1 (ChapterID) and column 3 (Title).  
      • From author_data.tsv   take column 2 (AuthorName).  
      • From release_data.tsv  take column 3 (TargetDate).  
   4. Keep the order shown in rule 2. Use TAB as the only delimiter, and end every line with a single LF.  
   5. The resulting file must have exactly four lines and look like the sample under the “Verification expectations” section below.

B. Produce a plain-text command journal located at /home/user/logs/merge_activities.log whose job is to document the column-manipulation steps you performed.  
   • The parent directory /home/user/logs/ must exist already; if it does not, create it.  
   • Every line of the journal must begin with the literal string “CMD: ” (note the space).  
   • Write exactly four journal lines in this order:  
     1. The command used to extract the desired columns from chapter_data.tsv.  
     2. The command used to extract the desired columns from author_data.tsv.  
     3. The command used to extract the desired columns from release_data.tsv.  
     4. The command used to paste/assemble the three temporary column streams into release_master.tsv.  
   • The lines in the journal are **not** expected to be executed by anybody; they serve only as documentation.  
   • Apart from the required “CMD: ” prefix, record each full command exactly as you would type it in Bash, including all flags, redirections, pipelines, and file paths.  
   • No extra commentary, blank lines, or trailing spaces are allowed in the journal.

C. Once everything is complete, double-check your work:  
   1. release_master.tsv must contain exactly 4 lines, 4 TAB-separated columns per data row, and match the sample shown below.  
   2. merge_activities.log must contain exactly 4 non-empty lines, each starting with “CMD: ”.  
   3. Both output files must be world-readable (chmod 644 is sufficient) but only user-writable.

Verification expectations  
(The automated tests will compare your artefacts byte-for-byte.)

Correct /home/user/documents/final/release_master.tsv (␉ denotes a single TAB):

ChapterID␉Title␉AuthorName␉TargetDate  
CH01␉Introduction␉Alice Johnson␉2023-10-01  
CH02␉Installation␉Bob Smith␉2023-10-15  
CH03␉Configuration␉Carol Chen␉2023-11-01  

Correct /home/user/logs/merge_activities.log must contain exactly four lines, each beginning with “CMD: ”, and no blank line at the end of file.  The specific command text is up to you, but the line count and the “CMD: ” prefix are mandatory and will be tested.

Hints (do NOT reveal actual commands; only conceptual guidance):
• “cut -f …” is handy for extracting specific columns.  
• “tail -n +2” or “sed 1d” can help you skip the header rows.  
• “paste” joins streams horizontally.  
• Temporary files may be useful; remember to clean up or ignore them as you wish.  
• Make sure every intermediate command succeeds before proceeding.

Goal: after you finish, the senior technical-writer can immediately open /home/user/documents/final/release_master.tsv and see the clean four-column table, and can read /home/user/logs/merge_activities.log to understand the exact cut & paste steps you performed.  No other output, directories, or files should be changed or created.

Files that must exist after completion:

CH01<TAB>Introduction<TAB>Alice Johnson<TAB>2023-10-01<LF>

Must have 4 lines ending with LF.
Each line must start with “CMD: ”.
Total line count = 4. We do NOT verify the exact commands, only:
   • Regular expression for every line: ^CMD:\s.+\n
No extra lines allowed.

No other files/directories should be created or modified.
